docs.title: FxSearchField
docs.text: Search-flavored text field — FxTextField preset with a leading search icon and an optional clear (×) button that appears while the field has text.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSearchField search = FxSearchField.builder()
    .promptText("Search users")
    .clearable(true)
    .onSearch(q -> viewModel.query(q))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Initial text. promptText(String) String "Search" Placeholder. variant(InputVariant) InputVariant DEFAULT Validation variant. helperText(String) String "" Helper line. errorText(String) String "" Error line. clearable(boolean) boolean false Show × when text is present. editable(boolean) boolean true Editable flag. onSearch(OnSearch) callback none Fires on ENTER with the current text.

docs.title: FxAutocomplete
docs.text: Text input with a suggestion popup — FxTextField plus a ListView in a Popup anchored below. Filters candidates against the field's text via a stringifier (default: Object::toString).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxAutocomplete<String> ac = FxAutocomplete.<String>builder()
    .items("apple", "apricot", "banana")
    .promptText("Fruit")
    .onSelect(fruit -> logger.info("chose {}", fruit))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description items(List<T>) / items(T...) items empty Candidate items. text(String) String "" Initial text. promptText(String) String "" Placeholder. variant(InputVariant) InputVariant DEFAULT Validation variant. helperText(String) String "" Helper line. errorText(String) String "" Error line. stringifier(Function<T, String>) fn Object::toString Renders + filters items. onSelect(OnSelect<T>) callback none Fires when a suggestion is chosen.

docs.title: FxTagInput
docs.text: Chip-based tag entry — a wrapping row of FxChip tags plus an inline text editor. Press ENTER to commit, BACKSPACE on empty to remove the last tag, click × on any chip to remove it.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxTagInput tags = FxTagInput.builder()
    .tags("java", "javafx")
    .promptText("Add tag…")
    .onChange(list -> viewModel.setTags(list))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description tags(List<String>) / tags(String...) tags empty Initial tag list. promptText(String) String "" Editor placeholder. variant(InputVariant) InputVariant DEFAULT Validation variant. helperText(String) String "" Helper line. errorText(String) String "" Error line. onChange(OnChange) callback none Fires when tag list changes.

docs.title: FxOTPInput
docs.text: One-time-code entry — a row of length single-character text boxes with auto-advance on type and clear-back on backspace.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxOTPInput otp = FxOTPInput.builder()
    .length(6)
    .digitsOnly(true)
    .onComplete(code -> viewModel.verify(code))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description length(int) int 6 Number of boxes. digitsOnly(boolean) boolean true Restrict input to [0-9]. initial(String) String "" Pre-fill values. onComplete(OnComplete) callback none Fires when every box is filled.

docs.title: FxMaskedInput
docs.text: Text input with a fixed input mask. Placeholder characters: # = digit, A = ASCII letter, * = any character. Any other mask character is a literal separator.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxMaskedInput phone = FxMaskedInput.builder()
    .mask("###-###-####")
    .promptText("555-123-4567")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description mask(String) String "" Mask pattern (#, A, *, literals). text(String) String "" Initial value. promptText(String) String "" Placeholder. variant(InputVariant) InputVariant DEFAULT Validation variant. helperText(String) String "" Helper line. errorText(String) String "" Error line.

docs.title: FxCodeEditor
docs.text: Code editor built on RichTextFX CodeArea. Optional line-number gutter, plus a highlighter callback (RichTextFX StyleSpans<Collection<String>> pattern) that re-tokenizes on text change.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxCodeEditor ed = FxCodeEditor.builder()
    .text("int x = 42;")
    .showLineNumbers(true)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Initial source. showLineNumbers(boolean) boolean true Line-number gutter. editable(boolean) boolean true Editable flag. highlighter(Function<String, StyleSpans<Collection<String>>>) fn none Syntax highlighter callback.

docs.title: FxRichTextEditor
docs.text: Rich-text editor built on RichTextFX InlineCssTextArea. Ships a bold / italic / underline toolbar that applies inline CSS to the current selection.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxRichTextEditor ed = FxRichTextEditor.builder()
    .text("Type here…")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Initial plain text. editable(boolean) boolean true Editable flag.

docs.title: FxMarkdownEditor
docs.text: Split source-editor + rendered-preview markdown editor. Preview renders a compact subset: #/##/### headings, blank-line paragraphs, - bullets, and inline **bold** / *italic* markers (strip-only).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxMarkdownEditor md = FxMarkdownEditor.builder()
    .text("# Hello\n\nSome **bold** text.")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Initial markdown source. dividerPosition(double) double 0.5 Split divider position in [0, 1].

docs.title: FxJsonEditor
docs.text: JSON-flavored FxCodeEditor — wraps a code editor preset with a regex-driven JSON tokenizer emitting CSS classes json-key, json-string, json-number, json-boolean, json-null, json-punctuation.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxJsonEditor ed = FxJsonEditor.builder()
    .text("{\"name\":\"Forja\",\"version\":0.1}")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Initial JSON source.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
Structural primitives — arrange other components.

Component Summary FxSeparator Horizontal or vertical divider line. FxSpacer Absorbs available space between siblings. FxStack Vertical flow with spacing. FxRow Horizontal flow with spacing. FxContainer Max-width centered container. FxSection Titled section wrapper. FxCard Framed content card with variant, header, and footer slots. FxGrid Fluent-builder GridPane wrapper. FxFlex Wrap-flow layout (FlowPane) with gap control. FxAspectRatio Constrains a single child to a fixed width:height ratio. FxScrollArea Styled ScrollPane wrapper. FxSplitView Styled resizable SplitPane. FxResizablePane Container with user-draggable edge handle. FxCollapse Single-section collapsible with header + expandable body. FxAccordion Stack of FxCollapse sections with optional single-open enforcement. FxStickyHeader Pins its header to the top of a host ScrollPane. FxResponsive Swaps children based on width breakpoints.

docs.title: FxSeparator
docs.text: Divider line. Horizontal or vertical, thin or bold.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSeparator hr = FxSeparator.builder()
    .orientation(Orientation.HORIZONTAL)
    .variant(SeparatorVariant.DEFAULT)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description orientation(Orientation) Orientation HORIZONTAL Line direction. variant(SeparatorVariant) SeparatorVariant DEFAULT Style variant.

docs.title: FxSpacer
docs.text: Absorbs available space between siblings inside an HBox / VBox. Ships as a Region with Priority.ALWAYS grow already set.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: HBox row = new HBox(leftNode, FxSpacer.builder().build(), rightNode);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description size(SpacingSize) SpacingSize NONE Optional minimum size along the axis.

docs.title: FxStack
docs.text: Vertical flow with spacing. Thin wrapper around VBox with a token-based spacing scale.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxStack stack = FxStack.builder()
    .spacing(SpacingSize.MD)
    .children(header, body, footer)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description spacing(SpacingSize) SpacingSize SM Gap between children. alignment(Pos) Pos TOP_LEFT Content alignment. children(Node...) Node[] empty Child nodes.

docs.title: FxRow
docs.text: Horizontal flow with spacing. Thin wrapper around HBox with a token-based spacing scale.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxRow row = FxRow.builder()
    .spacing(SpacingSize.SM)
    .children(icon, label, spacer, action)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description spacing(SpacingSize) SpacingSize SM Gap between children. alignment(Pos) Pos CENTER_LEFT Content alignment. children(Node...) Node[] empty Child nodes.

docs.title: FxContainer
docs.text: Max-width centered container. Use for page-level content wrappers that shouldn't stretch full-width on wide screens.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxContainer page = FxContainer.builder()
    .width(ContainerWidth.LG)
    .children(headerRow, mainSection)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description width(ContainerWidth) ContainerWidth MD Max width preset. children(Node...) Node[] empty Contained children.

docs.title: FxSection
docs.text: Titled section wrapper. Renders a section-heading label above a body slot.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSection settings = FxSection.builder()
    .title("Preferences")
    .children(themeToggle, densityToggle)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description title(String) String "" Section heading text. description(String) String "" Optional descriptive line under title. children(Node...) Node[] empty Body content.

docs.title: FxCard
docs.text: Framed content card. Slots for header, body, and footer, plus a CardVariant (DEFAULT / OUTLINED / ELEVATED).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxCard card = FxCard.builder()
    .variant(CardVariant.ELEVATED)
    .header(FxLabel.builder().text("Sales").variant(LabelVariant.HEADING).build())
    .body(FxText.builder().text("Q3 revenue up 12%.").build())
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description variant(CardVariant) CardVariant DEFAULT Visual variant. header(Node) Node none Header slot. body(Node) Node none Body slot. footer(Node) Node none Footer slot.

docs.title: FxGrid
docs.text: Fluent-builder GridPane wrapper. Add children with explicit (col, row) coordinates plus optional span.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxGrid form = FxGrid.builder()
    .hgap(12).vgap(8)
    .add(nameLabel, 0, 0).add(nameField, 1, 0)
    .add(emailLabel, 0, 1).add(emailField, 1, 1)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description hgap(double) double 0 Horizontal gap. vgap(double) double 0 Vertical gap. alignment(Pos) Pos TOP_LEFT Content alignment. add(Node, int, int) node + col + row — Add child at (col, row) with span 1x1. add(Node, int, int, int, int) node + col + row + colSpan + rowSpan — Add child with span.

docs.title: FxFlex
docs.text: Wrap-flow layout — a styled FlowPane. Children flow row-by-row (or column-by-column) and wrap to a new line when the axis fills.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxFlex tags = FxFlex.builder()
    .hgap(6).vgap(6)
    .children(tag1, tag2, tag3, tag4)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description orientation(Orientation) Orientation HORIZONTAL Flow direction. hgap(double) double 0 Horizontal gap. vgap(double) double 0 Vertical gap. gap(double) double 0 Sets both hgap and vgap. alignment(Pos) Pos TOP_LEFT Content alignment. children(Node...) Node[] empty Child nodes.

docs.title: FxAspectRatio
docs.text: Container that constrains its single child to a fixed width:height ratio. Takes the available width from its parent, computes height = width / ratio, and lays out the child.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxAspectRatio banner = FxAspectRatio.builder()
    .ratio(16.0 / 9.0)
    .child(imageView)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description ratio(double) double 1.0 Width / height ratio. child(Node) Node null Single child.

docs.title: FxScrollArea
docs.text: Styled ScrollPane wrapper. Content, viewport, and scrollbar APIs remain fully accessible.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxScrollArea sa = FxScrollArea.builder()
    .content(longVBox)
    .fitToWidth(true)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description content(Node) Node none Scrolled content. fitToWidth(boolean) boolean true Match content width to viewport. fitToHeight(boolean) boolean false Match content height to viewport. hbarPolicy(ScrollBarPolicy) ScrollBarPolicy AS_NEEDED Horizontal scroll bar policy. vbarPolicy(ScrollBarPolicy) ScrollBarPolicy AS_NEEDED Vertical scroll bar policy.

docs.title: FxSplitView
docs.text: Styled resizable split view — a SplitPane wrapper. Divider positioning and item management remain fully accessible.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSplitView editor = FxSplitView.builder()
    .items(fileTree, codeArea)
    .dividerPositions(0.25)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description orientation(Orientation) Orientation HORIZONTAL Split direction. items(Node...) Node[] empty Split children. dividerPositions(double...) double[] auto Initial divider positions in [0, 1].

docs.title: FxResizablePane
docs.text: Container whose single child can be resized along one edge by dragging a user-visible handle. Useful for sidebars and drawer-style panels that aren't full overlays.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxResizablePane sidebar = FxResizablePane.builder()
    .child(sidebarContent)
    .side(Side.RIGHT)
    .extent(240).minExtent(120).maxExtent(480)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description child(Node) Node none Single child. side(Side) Side RIGHT Which edge hosts the drag handle. extent(double) double 240 Child size along the drag axis. minExtent(double) double 80 Minimum extent. maxExtent(double) double MAX_VALUE Maximum extent.

docs.title: FxCollapse
docs.text: Single-section collapsible — a clickable header row plus an expandable body slot. The chevron rotates via the :expanded pseudo-class.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxCollapse details = FxCollapse.builder()
    .title("Details")
    .content(detailsBody)
    .expanded(false)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description title(String) String "" Header text. content(Node) Node none Expandable body node. expanded(boolean) boolean false Initial expanded state.

docs.title: FxAccordion
docs.text: Vertical stack of FxCollapse sections. When singleOpen is true, expanding one section collapses the others.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxAccordion faq = FxAccordion.builder()
    .sections(shipping, returns, contact)
    .singleOpen(true)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description sections(FxCollapse...) FxCollapse[] empty Section list. singleOpen(boolean) boolean true Enforce one-open-at-a-time.

docs.title: FxStickyHeader
docs.text: Pins its first child (the header) to the top of a host ScrollPane's viewport while the rest of the content scrolls underneath.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxStickyHeader sticky = FxStickyHeader.builder()
    .header(sectionTitle)
    .body(longContent)
    .build();
scrollPane.setContent(sticky);
sticky.attachTo(scrollPane);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description header(Node) Node none Pinned header. body(Node) Node none Scrolling body. attachTo(ScrollPane) ScrollPane none Wires the header's translateY to the scroll pane's vvalue.

docs.title: FxResponsive
docs.text: Responsive container — swaps its visible child based on the current width and a set of named breakpoints. Picks the entry with the greatest minWidth ≤ getWidth().

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxResponsive layout = FxResponsive.builder()
    .at("base", 0, mobileLayout)
    .at("md", 768, tabletLayout)
    .at("lg", 1024, desktopLayout)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description at(String, double, Node) name + minWidth + node — Register a breakpoint.

docs.title: Media
docs.text:
Video, audio, image gallery, waveform.

Requires the javafx-media module at runtime for video/audio playback. Waveform reads PCM via javax.sound.sampled (WAV/AU/AIFF supported without extra codecs).

Component Summary FxVideoPlayer Video player with play/pause, seek slider, and elapsed/duration label. FxAudioPlayer Compact audio player with play/pause and progress. FxImageGallery Thumbnail grid that opens FxLightbox on click. FxWaveform Canvas-drawn PCM waveform display.

docs.title: FxVideoPlayer
docs.text: Video player — MediaView plus a play/pause button, a seek slider, and an elapsed/duration label. Requires the javafx-media module at runtime.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxVideoPlayer vp = FxVideoPlayer.builder()
    .url("file:/path/to/video.mp4")
    .autoPlay(false)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description url(String) String "" Media URL. autoPlay(boolean) boolean false Start playing on build. fitWidth(double) double auto Sets MediaView.fitWidth. fitHeight(double) double auto Sets MediaView.fitHeight.

docs.title: FxAudioPlayer
docs.text: Compact audio player — play/pause + seek slider + elapsed/duration label. Requires the javafx-media module at runtime.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxAudioPlayer ap = FxAudioPlayer.builder()
    .url("file:/path/to/track.mp3")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description url(String) String "" Media URL. autoPlay(boolean) boolean false Start playing on build.

docs.title: FxImageGallery
docs.text: Grid of thumbnails that opens a full-screen FxLightbox on click. Backed by FxImage thumbnails that share the same URL list with the lightbox.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxImageGallery gallery = FxImageGallery.builder()
    .images("file:/a.jpg", "file:/b.jpg", "file:/c.jpg")
    .thumbnailSize(120)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description images(List<String>) / images(String...) items empty Image URLs. thumbnailSize(double) double 120 Thumbnail edge length in px.

docs.title: FxWaveform
docs.text:
Waveform display for PCM audio. Reads WAV/AU/AIFF via javax.sound.sampled, decimates to the canvas width, paints a symmetric peaks polyline.

MP3 and other compressed formats require an external decoder — pass raw PCM bytes via setPcmBytes or normalized samples via setSamples when decoding externally.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxWaveform w = FxWaveform.builder()
    .file(new File("track.wav"))
    .stroke(Color.web("#4F46E5"))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description file(File) File none Load a WAV/AU/AIFF file. url(URL) URL none Load from a URL. samples(double[]) double[] none Pre-normalized [-1, 1] samples. stroke(Color) Color indigo Line color. width(double) double 320 Preferred width. height(double) double 80 Preferred height.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
Wayfinding — how users move between views.

Component Summary FxTabs Styled TabPane wrapper. FxVerticalTabs Left-side vertical tabs. FxAppBar Top bar with leading slot, title, and trailing actions. FxSidebar Fixed-width vertical container for navigation content. FxSidebarNav Keyed nav-item list with icon + label and active state. FxBreadcrumbs Trail of clickable segments separated by /. FxStepper Numbered dots with active + done pseudo-classes and captions. FxPagination Prev/next arrows + numbered page buttons with ellipsis window. FxMenuBar Styled MenuBar wrapper. FxContextMenu Styled ContextMenu wrapper. FxDropdownMenu Text-first MenuButton wrapper. FxAnchorNav In-page jump link list backed by a ScrollPane. FxCommandPalette ⌘K-style command launcher on the overlay layer.

docs.title: FxTabs
docs.text: Styled tabbed pane built on javafx.scene.control.TabPane. Tab construction, selection, and drag/close behaviors remain fully accessible.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxTabs tabs = FxTabs.builder()
    .tabs(new Tab("Overview", overview), new Tab("Docs", docs))
    .side(Side.TOP)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description tabs(Tab...) Tab[] empty Tabs. side(Side) Side TOP Tab-header side. closingPolicy(TabClosingPolicy) policy UNAVAILABLE Tab close policy.

docs.title: FxVerticalTabs
docs.text: Vertical (LEFT-side) tabbed pane. Same as FxTabs with side defaulted to Side.LEFT and rotateGraphic on so tab titles read horizontally.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxVerticalTabs settings = FxVerticalTabs.builder()
    .tabs(new Tab("Profile", profile), new Tab("Billing", billing))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description tabs(Tab...) Tab[] empty Tabs. side(Side) Side LEFT Tab-header side.

docs.title: FxAppBar
docs.text: Top app bar with a leading slot (menu/back), a title, and a trailing action slot. Aliased as FxNavbar.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxAppBar bar = FxAppBar.builder()
    .leading(menuButton)
    .title("Forja Demo")
    .actions(themeToggle, settingsButton)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description leading(Node...) Node[] empty Leading-slot children. title(String) String "" Title text. actions(Node...) Node[] empty Trailing-actions children.

docs.title: FxSidebar
docs.text: Vertical container for navigation content — a styled VBox with a fixed default width.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSidebar side = FxSidebar.builder()
    .width(240)
    .children(sidebarNav, footer)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description width(double) double 240 Preferred width. spacing(double) double 6 Gap between children. children(Node...) Node[] empty Sidebar content.

docs.title: FxSidebarNav
docs.text: Vertical list of keyed nav items with icon + label and an active state. The currently-active item's row gets the :active pseudo-class.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSidebarNav nav = FxSidebarNav.builder()
    .item("home",     "Home",     "fth-home")
    .item("projects", "Projects", "fth-folder")
    .item("settings", "Settings", "fth-settings")
    .active("projects")
    .onSelect(key -> router.go(key))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description item(String, String, String) key + label + iconLiteral — Add a nav item. active(String) String null Initial active key. onSelect(OnSelect) callback none Fires when active key changes.

docs.title: FxBreadcrumbs
docs.text: Trail of clickable segments separated by /. Each non-last segment is an FxLink; the last is a muted label representing the current location.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxBreadcrumbs bc = FxBreadcrumbs.builder()
    .segments("Projects", "Forja", "Docs")
    .onSelect(idx -> router.upTo(idx))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description segments(List<String>) / segments(String...) items empty Trail segments. onSelect(OnSelect) callback none Fires when a segment is clicked.

docs.title: FxStepper
docs.text: Horizontal multi-step progress indicator — numbered dots + optional labels connected by lines. Active and completed dots get :active / :done pseudo-classes.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxStepper wizard = FxStepper.builder()
    .steps("Account", "Profile", "Review")
    .currentStep(1)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description steps(List<String>) / steps(String...) items empty Step captions. currentStep(int) int 0 Current step index.

docs.title: FxPagination
docs.text: Prev/next arrows + numbered page buttons. When totalPages > visiblePages, only a window around the current page is shown with ellipsis markers.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxPagination pg = FxPagination.builder()
    .totalPages(20)
    .currentPage(0)
    .visiblePages(7)
    .onPageChange(p -> viewModel.loadPage(p))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description totalPages(int) int 1 Total pages. currentPage(int) int 0 Zero-based current page. visiblePages(int) int 7 Window size. onPageChange(OnPageChange) callback none Fires when current page changes.

docs.title: FxMenuBar
docs.text: Styled MenuBar wrapper.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxMenuBar mb = FxMenuBar.builder()
    .menus(fileMenu, editMenu, viewMenu)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description menus(Menu...) Menu[] empty Menus. useSystemMenuBar(boolean) boolean false Use the native system menu bar on macOS.

docs.title: FxContextMenu
docs.text: Styled ContextMenu wrapper. Install on a node via setOnContextMenuRequested.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxContextMenu cm = FxContextMenu.builder()
    .items(copy, paste, delete)
    .build();
target.setOnContextMenuRequested(e -> cm.show(target, e.getScreenX(), e.getScreenY()));

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description items(MenuItem...) MenuItem[] empty Menu items.

docs.title: FxDropdownMenu
docs.text: Text-first dropdown menu built on javafx.scene.control.MenuButton. Use FxMenuButton for the icon-only kebab variant.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxDropdownMenu m = FxDropdownMenu.builder()
    .text("Actions")
    .items(rename, duplicate, delete)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Button label. items(MenuItem...) MenuItem[] empty Menu items.

docs.title: FxAnchorNav
docs.text: Vertical list of in-page jump links backed by a ScrollPane + target-node map. Clicking a link scrolls the paired scroll pane so the target node's top aligns with the viewport top. Register the active key to highlight the current section.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxAnchorNav toc = FxAnchorNav.builder()
    .scrollPane(scrollPane)
    .section("intro",   "Intro",   introNode)
    .section("install", "Install", installNode)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description scrollPane(ScrollPane) ScrollPane none Host scroll pane. section(String, String, Node) key + label + target — Register a jump target. active(String) String null Initial active key.

docs.title: FxCommandPalette
docs.text: ⌘K-style command launcher — a centered floating FxAutocomplete over a command list, rendered on the OverlayHost. Optionally installs a scene-level KeyCombination accelerator (default Ctrl/Cmd+K).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxCommandPalette p = FxCommandPalette.builder()
    .command(new Command("newProject", "New project", "fth-plus", c -> ...))
    .command(new Command("openDocs",   "Open docs",   "fth-book", c -> ...))
    .accelerator(new KeyCodeCombination(KeyCode.K, KeyCombination.SHORTCUT_DOWN))
    .buildPalette();
p.install(scene);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text:
Method Type Default Description command(Command) Command — Register a single command. commands(Command...) Command[] empty Register a batch of commands. accelerator(KeyCombination) key combo Ctrl/Cmd+K Scene-level open accelerator.

Note: use buildPalette() to get the FxCommandPalette instance. The default build() returns the palette's outer StackPane for compatibility with the FxNodeBuilder contract but is deprecated.

docs.title: Overlays
docs.text:
Anything that renders above the app. Most ride on OverlayHost — a per-scene overlay layer installed on first use.

Component Summary FxTooltip Styled Tooltip with fluent builder and install(node, text) shortcut. FxDialog Modal dialog on the overlay layer with title, body, and footer slots. FxConfirmDialog Preset dialog with cancel + confirm buttons and boolean callback. FxFormDialog Preset dialog with save + cancel and optional canSave gate. FxPopover Floating panel anchored to a target node. FxHoverCard Popover driven by target hover with open/close delays. FxDrawer Side-anchored sliding panel with scrim. FxBottomSheet Bottom-anchored slide-up sheet with grabber handle. FxLightbox Full-scrim media viewer with prev/next navigation.

docs.title: FxTooltip
docs.text: Styled Tooltip with a fluent builder. Attach via FxTooltip.install(node, text) for any Node, or install(control, tooltip) for a Control.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxTooltip help = FxTooltip.builder()
    .text("Deletes this project permanently")
    .showDelayMs(300)
    .build();
FxTooltip.install(deleteButton, help);

// Or shortcut on any Node
FxTooltip.install(myNode, "Info");

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Tooltip text. showDelayMs(long) long 400 Delay before showing. showDurationMs(long) long 5000 Max on-screen duration. hideDelayMs(long) long 200 Delay before hiding.

docs.title: FxDialog
docs.text: Modal dialog rendered on the OverlayHost overlay layer. Contains a translucent scrim and a centered panel with title, body, and footer slots.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxDialog d = FxDialog.builder()
    .title("Rename project")
    .body(nameField)
    .footer(cancelButton, saveButton)
    .build();
d.show(scene);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description title(String) String "" Header title. body(Node...) Node[] empty Body content. footer(Node...) Node[] empty Footer children (usually buttons). dismissOnScrimClick(boolean) boolean true Close on scrim click. dismissOnEscape(boolean) boolean true Close on ESC. showCloseIcon(boolean) boolean true Show the header × icon. okOnly(String) String — Sugar: footer becomes one PRIMARY button that closes the dialog.

docs.title: FxConfirmDialog
docs.text: Confirmation dialog — an FxDialog preset with title, message, cancel + confirm buttons, and a boolean callback.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
// Static shortcut
FxConfirmDialog.ask(scene, "Delete project?",
    "This action cannot be undone.",
    confirmed -> { if (confirmed) doDelete(); });

// Or builder for custom labels + variant
FxConfirmDialog d = FxConfirmDialog.builder()
    .title("Delete project?")
    .message("This action cannot be undone.")
    .cancelText("Keep it")
    .confirmText("Delete")
    .confirmVariant(ButtonVariant.DANGER)
    .onResult(confirmed -> ...)
    .build();
d.show(scene);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description title(String) String "Confirm" Dialog title. message(String) String "" Body message. cancelText(String) String "Cancel" Cancel button label. confirmText(String) String "Confirm" Confirm button label. confirmVariant(ButtonVariant) ButtonVariant PRIMARY Confirm button variant. onResult(Consumer<Boolean>) callback none Fires with true = confirm, false = cancel.

docs.title: FxFormDialog
docs.text: Form-entry dialog — wraps an FxDialog with a body area, cancel button, and a save button whose enabled state is optionally driven by a validator Supplier<Boolean>.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxFormDialog dlg = FxFormDialog.builder()
    .title("Rename")
    .body(nameField)
    .saveText("Save")
    .canSave(() -> !nameField.getText().isEmpty())
    .onSubmit(() -> project.rename(nameField.getText()))
    .build();
dlg.show(scene);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description title(String) String "Form" Dialog title. body(Node) Node empty region Form body. cancelText(String) String "Cancel" Cancel button label. saveText(String) String "Save" Save button label. saveVariant(ButtonVariant) ButtonVariant PRIMARY Save button variant. canSave(Supplier<Boolean>) supplier none Gates save-button enabled state. onSubmit(OnSubmit) callback none Fires on save when canSave passes.

docs.title: FxPopover
docs.text: Floating panel anchored to a target Node. Wraps a Popup sized to arbitrary content and positioned on any Side of its anchor. Auto-hides on outside click.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxPopover pop = FxPopover.builder()
    .anchor(button)
    .content(menuBox)
    .side(Side.BOTTOM)
    .build();
pop.show();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description anchor(Node) Node none Anchor node. content(Node) Node none Popover content. side(Side) Side BOTTOM Side to appear on. autoHide(boolean) boolean true Hide on outside click.

docs.title: FxHoverCard
docs.text: Hover-triggered rich card. Wraps an FxPopover whose show/hide is driven by the target node's hoverProperty with configurable open/close delays.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxHoverCard hc = FxHoverCard.builder()
    .target(avatar)
    .content(profileCard)
    .openDelayMs(400)
    .closeDelayMs(200)
    .build();
// autoInstall=true wires listeners immediately; pass autoInstall(false) + call hc.install() later.

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description target(Node) Node none Node whose hover drives the card. content(Node) Node none Card content. side(Side) Side BOTTOM Side to appear on. openDelayMs(long) long 400 Delay before showing. closeDelayMs(long) long 200 Delay before hiding. autoInstall(boolean) boolean true Install hover listeners at build time.

docs.title: FxDrawer
docs.text: Side-anchored sliding panel rendered on OverlayHost. Opening slides the panel in via a translate transition; closing slides it back out. Scrim absorbs clicks; ESC closes.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxDrawer settings = FxDrawer.builder()
    .content(settingsPanel)
    .side(Side.RIGHT)
    .size(320)
    .build();
settings.open(scene);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description side(Side) Side RIGHT Anchor side. size(double) double 320 Panel size along the drawer's axis. content(Node) Node none Panel content.

docs.title: FxBottomSheet
docs.text: Bottom-anchored slide-up sheet with a grabber handle. Slides up from the scene bottom on open, slides back down on close.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxBottomSheet sheet = FxBottomSheet.builder()
    .content(sheetBody)
    .height(280)
    .build();
sheet.open(scene);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description content(Node) Node none Sheet content. height(double) double 280 Sheet height in px.

docs.title: FxLightbox
docs.text: Full-scrim media viewer for one or more image URLs. Navigation arrows step through the images; ESC closes; LEFT/RIGHT navigate.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxLightbox lb = FxLightbox.builder()
    .images("file:/a.png", "file:/b.png", "file:/c.png")
    .index(0)
    .build();
lb.open(scene);

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description images(List<String>) / images(String...) items empty Image URLs. index(int) int 0 Initial image index.

docs.title: Selection
docs.text:
Choice controls — pick one, pick many, pick a number, pick a color.

Component Summary FxCheckBox Styled checkbox with optional indeterminate state. FxCheckGroup Multi-select group of checkboxes with observable selected-values list. FxRadioGroup Single-select radio group with observable value property. FxSwitch Pill-style on/off toggle switch. FxSlider Numeric range slider with optional value label. FxComboBox Styled dropdown combo box. FxMultiSelect Multi-select combo with checkable items and N-selected summary. FxRangeSlider Two-thumb slider for numeric range selection. FxRating Star-rating input with hover preview. FxColorPicker Styled color picker.

docs.title: FxCheckBox
docs.text: Styled checkbox built on javafx.scene.control.CheckBox. Optional indeterminate state.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxCheckBox agree = FxCheckBox.builder()
    .text("I agree to the terms")
    .selected(false)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Label. selected(boolean) boolean false Initial selected state. indeterminate(boolean) boolean false Initial indeterminate state. allowIndeterminate(boolean) boolean false Allow the indeterminate state. onAction(EventHandler<ActionEvent>) handler none Click callback.

docs.title: FxCheckGroup
docs.text: Multi-select group of FxCheckBox items. Exposes an observable list of selected values and an optional heading label.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxCheckGroup toppings = FxCheckGroup.builder()
    .label("Toppings")
    .items("Cheese", "Pepperoni", "Mushrooms")
    .selected("Cheese")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description items(List<String>) / items(String...) items empty Option labels. selected(List<String>) / selected(String...) items empty Initially selected values. orientation(Orientation) Orientation VERTICAL Row or column layout. label(String) String "" Optional heading label.

docs.title: FxRadioGroup
docs.text: Single-select radio group. Radio buttons share a ToggleGroup; the current selection is exposed as a String value.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxRadioGroup size = FxRadioGroup.builder()
    .label("Size")
    .items("S", "M", "L")
    .value("M")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description items(List<String>) / items(String...) items empty Option labels. value(String) String null Initially selected value. orientation(Orientation) Orientation VERTICAL Row or column. label(String) String "" Optional heading label.

docs.title: FxSwitch
docs.text: Pill-style on/off toggle switch. Clicking the track flips the state; the knob slides via CSS transitions on the :selected pseudo-class. Keyboard: SPACE toggles when focused.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSwitch dark = FxSwitch.builder()
    .text("Dark mode")
    .selected(true)
    .onChange(v -> theme.setDark(v))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Optional label. selected(boolean) boolean false Initial state. onChange(OnChange) callback none Fires when selected changes.

docs.title: FxSlider
docs.text: Numeric range slider with optional value label + prefix/suffix. Value label auto-formats with the configured decimals.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSlider volume = FxSlider.builder()
    .min(0).max(100)
    .value(60).step(5)
    .suffix("%")
    .showValue(true)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description min(double) double 0 Minimum value. max(double) double 100 Maximum value. value(double) double 0 Initial value. step(double) double 1 Block increment. decimals(int) int 0 Fractional digits for value label. prefix(String) String "" Value-label prefix. suffix(String) String "" Value-label suffix. showValue(boolean) boolean false Show trailing value label. showTickMarks(boolean) boolean false Show tick marks. showTickLabels(boolean) boolean false Show tick labels. majorTickUnit(double) double 25 Major tick unit.

docs.title: FxComboBox
docs.text: Styled dropdown combo box built on javafx.scene.control.ComboBox<T>.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxComboBox<String> country = FxComboBox.<String>builder()
    .items("Canada", "USA", "Mexico")
    .value("Canada")
    .promptText("Select country")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description items(List<T>) / items(T...) items empty Options. value(T) T null Initial value. promptText(String) String "" Placeholder. editable(boolean) boolean false Editable text field. visibleRowCount(int) int 10 Dropdown row cap.

docs.title: FxMultiSelect
docs.text: Multi-select combo — a summary row + a dropdown popup of checkable items. Summary shows the prompt when empty, a single value when one is picked, or N selected for multiple.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxMultiSelect ms = FxMultiSelect.builder()
    .items("bug", "docs", "enhancement")
    .selected("docs")
    .promptText("Labels")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description items(List<String>) / items(String...) items empty Options. selected(List<String>) / selected(String...) items empty Initially selected values. promptText(String) String "" Summary shown when nothing selected.

docs.title: FxRangeSlider
docs.text: Two-thumb range slider. Implemented as two overlaid Sliders; the low thumb is clamped to ≤ highValue and the high thumb to ≥ lowValue.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxRangeSlider price = FxRangeSlider.builder()
    .min(0).max(1000)
    .lowValue(100).highValue(500)
    .step(10)
    .prefix("$")
    .showValues(true)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description min(double) double 0 Minimum. max(double) double 100 Maximum. lowValue(double) double 0 Low thumb value. highValue(double) double 100 High thumb value. step(double) double 1 Block increment. showValues(boolean) boolean false Show trailing "low – high" label. prefix(String) String "" Value-label prefix. suffix(String) String "" Value-label suffix.

docs.title: FxRating
docs.text: Star-rating input. Click a star to set the rating; hover previews highlight up to that position. Set readonly to display without interaction.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxRating stars = FxRating.builder()
    .max(5)
    .value(3)
    .onChange(v -> logger.info("rated {}", v))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description max(int) int 5 Star count. value(int) int 0 Initial rating, clamped to [0, max]. readonly(boolean) boolean false Disable interaction. onChange(OnChange) callback none Fires when value changes.

docs.title: FxColorPicker
docs.text: Styled color picker built on javafx.scene.control.ColorPicker. Everything native remains accessible.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxColorPicker cp = FxColorPicker.builder()
    .value(Color.web("#4F46E5"))
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description value(Color) Color Color.BLACK Initial color.

docs.title: Typography
docs.text:
Text-rendering primitives — labels, links, code blocks, keyboard chips, block quotes, bullet lists.

Component Summary FxLabel Themed label with LabelVariant (display / heading / subheading / body / small / mono). FxLink Text hyperlink with hover state and optional graphic. FxText Body text node with variant control. FxBlockquote Left-bar block quote for pulled-out prose. FxKbd Rendered keyboard-key chip (⌘, K, etc.). FxCode Inline monospaced code snippet. FxBulletList Ordered or unordered list of text items.

docs.title: FxLabel
docs.text: Themed label built on javafx.scene.control.Label. Selects a size + weight combo via LabelVariant (DISPLAY / HEADING / SUBHEADING / BODY / SMALL / MONO) and adds a muted-color pseudo-class.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxLabel title = FxLabel.builder()
    .text("Projects")
    .variant(LabelVariant.HEADING)
    .build();

FxLabel helper = FxLabel.builder()
    .text("Optional")
    .variant(LabelVariant.SMALL)
    .muted(true)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Label text. variant(LabelVariant) LabelVariant BODY Typography variant. muted(boolean) boolean false Applies the muted color pseudo-class. wrapText(boolean) boolean false Enable text wrap.

docs.title: FxLink
docs.text: Text hyperlink. Extends javafx.scene.control.Hyperlink with a variant enum and an optional leading/trailing graphic.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxLink docs = FxLink.builder()
    .text("Read the docs")
    .variant(LinkVariant.BODY)
    .onAction(e -> openDocs())
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Link text. variant(LinkVariant) LinkVariant BODY Size variant. graphic(Node) Node none Optional graphic (typically an FxIcon). onAction(EventHandler<ActionEvent>) handler none Click callback.

docs.title: FxText
docs.text: Body text node with variant control. Use it when you need paragraphs of text with typography controls that don't fit FxLabel's single-line default.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxText body = FxText.builder()
    .text("Forja layers a design system on top of JavaFX...")
    .variant(TextVariant.BODY)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Text content. variant(TextVariant) TextVariant BODY Size + weight variant. wrapText(boolean) boolean true Wrap long text.

docs.title: FxBlockquote
docs.text: Pulled-out prose block with a left accent bar. Wraps a Label inside a bordered container.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxBlockquote quote = FxBlockquote.builder()
    .text("Shape what already works.")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Quoted text. attribution(String) String "" Optional attribution line.

docs.title: FxKbd
docs.text: Rendered keyboard-key chip. Use inside prose or a FxKeybindingHint row to reference specific keys.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxKbd cmd = FxKbd.builder().text("⌘").build();
FxKbd k   = FxKbd.builder().text("K").build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Key label.

docs.title: FxCode
docs.text: Inline monospaced code snippet — a chip with a JetBrains Mono font.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: FxCode ref = FxCode.builder().text("Forja.install(scene)").build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Code text.

docs.title: FxBulletList
docs.text: Ordered or unordered vertical list. Uses • for UNORDERED and 1., 2., ... for ORDERED.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxBulletList steps = FxBulletList.builder()
    .kind(FxBulletList.Kind.ORDERED)
    .items("Install", "Configure", "Deploy")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description kind(Kind) Kind UNORDERED UNORDERED (•) or ORDERED (1., 2., ...). items(List<String>) List<String> empty List items. items(String...) String[] empty Varargs form.

docs.title: Utilities
docs.text:
Cross-cutting helpers that don't fit in a category.

Component Summary FxIcon Styled icon glyph built on Ikonli (bundles the Feather pack). FxThemeToggle Button that toggles a scene between light and dark themes. FxKeybindingHint Row of FxKbd chips separated by + (e.g. ⌘ + K). FxSearchHighlight TextFlow that bolds occurrences of a query in a source string. FxScrollSpy Tracks which registered section is visible inside a ScrollPane. FxPortal Renders a node into the overlay layer on demand. FxFocusTrap Cycles keyboard focus among a subtree's descendants.

docs.title: FxIcon
docs.text: Styled icon glyph built on Ikonli's FontIcon. Renders glyphs from any Ikonli pack on the classpath (Feather is bundled by default). Adds a semantic color variant system tied to the active theme.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxIcon save = FxIcon.builder()
    .literal("fth-save")
    .size(20)
    .variant(SemanticVariant.ACCENT)
    .build();

// Or plain constructor
FxIcon save2 = new FxIcon("fth-save");

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description literal(String) String "" Ikonli icon literal (e.g. fth-save). size(int) int 16 Icon size in px. variant(SemanticVariant) SemanticVariant DEFAULT Color variant.

docs.title: FxThemeToggle
docs.text: Button that toggles between FxTheme.LIGHT and FxTheme.DARK on a target Scene via Forja.applyTheme. Icon reflects current theme — sun in dark mode, moon in light mode.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxThemeToggle toggle = FxThemeToggle.builder()
    .scene(scene)
    .initial(FxTheme.LIGHT)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description scene(Scene) Scene null Target scene the toggle applies to. initial(FxTheme) FxTheme LIGHT Starting theme.

docs.title: FxKeybindingHint
docs.text: Row of FxKbd chips separated by a small + glyph — a compact way to display a keyboard shortcut like ⌘ + K.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxKeybindingHint hint = FxKeybindingHint.builder()
    .keys("⌘", "K")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description keys(List<String>) / keys(String...) items empty Key labels.

docs.title: FxSearchHighlight
docs.text: TextFlow that renders a source string with occurrences of a search query wrapped in bold Text nodes so matches stand out. Substring match (not regex).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxSearchHighlight hl = FxSearchHighlight.builder()
    .text("Deploy the pipeline to production")
    .query("pipe")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description text(String) String "" Source text. query(String) String "" Query to highlight. caseSensitive(boolean) boolean false Case-sensitive matching.

docs.title: FxScrollSpy
docs.text: Non-visual controller that tracks which of several registered "section" nodes is currently in view inside a ScrollPane. Exposes the active section's key via activeKeyProperty().

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxScrollSpy spy = FxScrollSpy.builder()
    .scrollPane(scrollPane)
    .section("intro",     intro)
    .section("install",   install)
    .section("api",       api)
    .build();
spy.activeKeyProperty().addListener((obs, o, key) -> sidebar.highlight(key));

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description scrollPane(ScrollPane) ScrollPane none Host scroll pane. section(String, Node) key + node — Register a section.

docs.title: FxPortal
docs.text:
"Render-elsewhere" helper — attaches a target node to the OverlayHost overlay layer of a given Scene whenever visible is true, and removes it when false.

Useful for lightbox, dropdown-menu, or popup content that logically belongs to a component but should render on top of the whole scene.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxPortal portal = FxPortal.builder()
    .scene(scene)
    .node(mySnackbar)
    .position(Pos.BOTTOM_CENTER)
    .visible(true)
    .build();

portal.setVisible(false); // detaches from overlay

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description scene(Scene) Scene null Target scene. node(Node) Node null Node to portal into the overlay. position(Pos) Pos CENTER Alignment on the overlay layer. visible(boolean) boolean false Initial attach state.

docs.title: FxFocusTrap
docs.text:
Non-visual utility that traps keyboard focus inside a root node's subtree. Intercepts TAB / SHIFT+TAB and cycles focus among focusable descendants.

Typical use: overlay dialogs — install on the dialog's panel so users can't tab out into the underlying app.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxFocusTrap trap = FxFocusTrap.builder()
    .root(dialogPanel)
    .build();
// autoInstall=true (default) wires the listener immediately.

// later
trap.uninstall();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description root(Parent) Parent null Root subtree to trap focus in. autoInstall(boolean) boolean true Attach key-listener at build time.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
Declarative form binding and a rule engine. FxValidator is pure logic — no scene dependencies — and can be reused outside of a form.

Component Summary FxValidator Sequential rule engine with built-in required / email / range / matches factories. FxFormField Label + control + error slot with :invalid / :required pseudo-classes. FxErrorSummary Aggregate danger-colored list of validation errors. FxForm Declarative form container that validates every field in one call.

docs.title: FxValidator
docs.text:
Sequential rule engine for validating a single value against a chain of Rule<T>s. Runs rules in order, returns on the first non-null error message. Pure logic — no scene dependencies — safe to unit test.

Built-in rule factories: required(), minLength(), maxLength(), matches(), email(), range().

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxValidator<String> emailValidator = FxValidator.<String>builder()
    .rule(FxValidator.required())
    .rule(FxValidator.email("Must be a valid email"))
    .build();
String err = emailValidator.validate("bad"); // "Must be a valid email"

// Or convenience
FxValidator<String> v = FxValidator.of(FxValidator.required(), FxValidator.email("Invalid"));

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description rule(Rule<T>) Rule<T> — Append a single rule. rules(Rule<T>...) Rule<T>[] empty Append multiple rules.

docs.title: Static factories
docs.text: Factory Returns Rejects required() Rule<String> null / blank required(String) Rule<String> null / blank (custom message) minLength(int, String) Rule<String> shorter than min maxLength(int, String) Rule<String> longer than max matches(String, String) Rule<String> doesn't match regex email(String) Rule<String> doesn't look like email range(double, double, String) Rule<Double> outside [min, max]

docs.title: FxFormField
docs.text: Form-field row — label + control + error slot. Set a validator to plug an FxValidator into the field; call validate() to compute the error against a value read from the supplier. Applies :invalid / :required pseudo-classes.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxFormField<String> emailField = FxFormField.<String>builder()
    .label("Email")
    .control(emailInput)
    .validator(FxValidator.<String>of(FxValidator.required(), FxValidator.email("Invalid email")))
    .valueSupplier(emailInput::getText)
    .required(true)
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description label(String) String "" Field label. control(Node) Node none Wrapped control. validator(FxValidator<T>) validator none Rule engine. valueSupplier(Supplier<T>) supplier none Reads current value on validate(). required(boolean) boolean false Adds * marker + :required pseudo-class.

docs.title: FxErrorSummary
docs.text: Aggregate list of validation errors, framed as a danger-colored panel. Auto-hides when the errors list is empty.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxErrorSummary summary = FxErrorSummary.builder()
    .title("Please fix the following:")
    .errors("Email is required", "Password too short")
    .build();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description title(String) String "There were errors" Header title. errors(List<String>) / errors(String...) items empty Error messages.

docs.title: FxForm
docs.text: Declarative form container — vertical stack of FxFormFields with an optional FxErrorSummary at the top. validate() runs every field's validator, populates each field's error, and fills the summary with all currently-invalid messages.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
FxForm form = FxForm.builder()
    .field(emailField)
    .field(passwordField)
    .showSummary(true)
    .build();
if (form.validate()) submit();

docs.title: Builder methods
docs.text: Method Type Default Description field(FxFormField<?>) field — Add a single field. fields(FxFormField<?>...) fields empty Add multiple fields. showSummary(boolean) boolean true Include the FxErrorSummary at top. summaryTitle(String) String "Please fix the following:" Summary panel title.
